text: To prove that span{u, v} = span{u + v, 4 — v}, we need to show that 4, v € span{t + v, — v} and & + ¥, % — v € span{,v}. Itis clear the & — v, u + v € span{4, v} since & — v and % + v are linear combinations of % and v. It follows that span{t + v,% — v} C span{i, v}. The vector @ is in span{t + @, i — v} since t = (4 + ¥) + 3 (& — v). Similarly ¥ € span{t + ¥, % — v} since ¥ = % (i + ) + %(i — ). It follows that span{u, v} C span{i + 9,4 — v}. Hence span{u, v} = span{i + v,u — v}.
